expectations.text: outputs/repo/docs/release.md includes the new release sanity command.
expectations.evidence:
# Release process

1. Update rollout manifests.
2. Run `./scripts/check-release`.
3. Run `python3 scripts/release_sanity.py`.
4. Tag the release.

expectations.text: outputs/repo/AGENTS.md still links release detail to docs/release.md and does not inline the new command.
expectations.evidence:
# AGENTS.md

- Release process details live in `docs/release.md`.
- Keep root guidance short and link to detailed docs instead of copying long checklists here.

expectations.text: outputs/report.md includes Learnings and Applied updates.
expectations.evidence:
## Learnings

- Qualified: After editing rollout manifests, run `python3 scripts/release_sanity.py`.
- Qualified: Keep release-process detail in `docs/release.md` (not root `AGENTS.md`) because root already delegates that topic.
- Not qualified: Temporary scratch-file note (`notes.txt`) is one-off and not durable guidance.

## Applied updates

- Copied fixture repo to `outputs/repo/`.
- Updated `outputs/repo/docs/release.md` to add `python3 scripts/release_sanity.py` as a release step after `./scripts/check-release`.
- Left `outputs/repo/AGENTS.md` unchanged to preserve linked-doc structure.
